title: World History: Year 1 to 2026
description: Important incidents and events organized by century

events.title: Birth of Christianity
events.description: Jesus of Nazareth is crucified in Jerusalem (~30-33 AD), leading to the foundation of Christianity

events.title: Eruption of Mount Vesuvius
events.description: Destroys Pompeii and Herculaneum, killing thousands.

events.title: Roman Empire reaches greatest extent under Trajan
events.description: Trajan becomes Emperor, initiating maximum territorial expansion.

events.title: Spread of Buddhism to China
events.description: Buddhist missionaries arrive in China via the Silk Road.
events.source: Hou Hanshu (Book of the Later Han)

events.title: Invention of paper in China
events.description: Cai Lun refines papermaking using mulberry bark, hemp, and rags.
events.source: Hou Hanshu (Book of the Later Han)

events.title: Five Good Emperors of Rome
events.description: Nerva, Trajan, Hadrian, Antoninus Pius, Marcus Aurelius rule in succession.
events.source: Edward Gibbon, Decline and Fall; Cassius Dio

events.title: Bar Kokhba Revolt
events.description: Jewish rebellion in Judea crushed by Hadrian. Judea renamed Syria Palaestina.

events.title: Hadrian's Wall constructed
events.description: Defensive wall across northern Britain, 73 miles long.

events.title: Spread of Christianity
events.description: By 200 AD, Christian communities exist in every major Roman city.

events.title: Han Dynasty begins collapse
events.description: Yellow Turban Rebellion weakens Han Dynasty, leading to Three Kingdoms.

events.title: Crisis of the Third Century
events.description: Roman Empire nearly collapses from civil war, plague, and barbarian invasions. Over 20 emperors in 50 years.

events.title: Three Kingdoms Period in China
events.description: China split into Wei, Shu, and Wu. Ends with Jin Dynasty reunification.

events.title: Goths invade Roman Empire
events.description: Emperor Decius killed at Battle of Abritus, first Roman Emperor to die fighting barbarians.

events.title: Diocletian becomes Emperor
events.description: Establishes the Tetrarchy, restores stability, launches last great Christian persecution.

events.title: Maya Classic Period begins
events.description: Major Maya city-states rise with advanced writing, astronomy, and architecture.
events.source: Martin & Grube, Chronicle of the Maya Kings and Queens
